problem.title: Inorder Successor in BST
problem.description: Given the root of a binary search tree and a node p in it, return the in-order successor of that node in the BST.
assumptions: In-order successor = next node in in-order traversal (next larger value).

example.explanation: In-order of BST: 1,2,3. Successor of 1 is 2 (next larger value).

solutions.title: Solution 1 – BST Traversal
solutions.description: Exploit the BST ordering property: if the current node's value is greater than p's value, it is a candidate successor and we move left to find a potentially smaller one; otherwise we move right because the successor must be in the right subtree. The last candidate recorded before falling off the tree is the answer. The key insight is that we never need to visit the entire tree — the BST property guides us directly to the successor in O(h) time.
